evals.prompt: I have 200 support tickets and I need to classify each one by category and priority using DSPy. It is too slow doing them one at a time.
evals.expected_output: Uses dspy.Parallel or .batch() to process tickets concurrently, not a sequential for loop

assertions.name: uses_parallel_execution
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Parallel or module.batch() with a num_threads parameter, not a sequential for loop for the main batch

assertions.name: builds_exec_pairs_or_examples
assertions.description: Either builds exec_pairs as list of (module, dict) tuples for Parallel, or builds a list of dspy.Example for .batch()

assertions.name: results_in_input_order
assertions.description: Accesses results by index or zip with original inputs, demonstrating order is preserved

assertions.name: provider_agnostic
assertions.description: LM config uses generic provider (e.g. openai/gpt-4o-mini) with a comment showing alternatives

evals.prompt: I want to analyze a document from three angles at once: sentiment, topic extraction, and entity extraction. How do I run all three DSPy modules in parallel on the same input?
evals.expected_output: Fan-out pattern using dspy.Parallel with three different modules on the same input text

assertions.name: uses_dspy_parallel_not_batch
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Parallel (not .batch()) since different modules are needed per pair

assertions.name: builds_exec_pairs_with_different_modules
assertions.description: Creates exec_pairs with three different module instances (one per analysis type) but the same input text

assertions.name: accesses_results_by_index
assertions.description: Accesses results[0], results[1], results[2] to get each analysis separately

evals.prompt: My parallel DSPy batch keeps failing partway through because some inputs cause errors. I want to get partial results and retry just the failures.
evals.expected_output: Uses return_failed_examples=True and destructures the 3-tuple return value

assertions.name: sets_return_failed_examples
assertions.description: Sets return_failed_examples=True in the Parallel constructor or .batch() call

assertions.name: destructures_three_tuple
assertions.description: Destructures the return as a 3-tuple: results, failed_examples, exceptions — not just a flat list

assertions.name: shows_retry_pattern
assertions.description: Demonstrates retrying failed examples (e.g., second pass or fallback module)

assertions.name: uses_max_errors
assertions.description: Sets max_errors to control when to abort the batch entirely
